intro: Orthophytum glabrum, also known as the Brazilian air plant, is a type of succulent plant that belongs to the Bromeliaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can aid in wound healing, reducing pain and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Orthophytum glabrum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthraquinones, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
